name: US Corn Belt
climate: Humid continental; hot summers, cold winters; adequate rainfall for rain-fed agriculture; increasing variability due to climate change

neighbors.name: US Rust Belt
neighbors.relevance: Adjacent industrial region; farm equipment manufacturing and agricultural processing overlap

neighbors.name: USMCA
neighbors.relevance: Corn Belt is a core USMCA agricultural engine; corn and soybean exports to Mexico and Canada

neighbors.name: Silicon Valley
neighbors.relevance: Ag-tech innovation (precision farming, satellite monitoring) increasingly drives Corn Belt productivity

neighbors.name: Mercosur
neighbors.relevance: Brazil and Argentina are direct soybean and corn competitors in global export markets

links.description: Corn Belt sits within USMCA territory; Mexico is the top export market for US corn
Great Lakes industrial corridor processes Corn Belt output; ethanol plants and food processing
Gulf export terminals ship grain through the Panama Canal to Asian buyers
Brazil and US compete for global soybean market share; trade war shifts redirect flows
RCEP members (China, Japan, South Korea) are top destinations for US corn and soybean exports
G20 agricultural trade policies and biofuel mandates directly affect Corn Belt commodity prices
